transcript.clean_text: come on hello and welcome to the 17th and penultimate stage of the 20 20 buelta it was back into the mountains for the peleton before tomorrow's processional final stage through the streets of Madrid the podium places weren't totally settled though 24 hours from the end of this year's race and with six climbs on the 178 km stage there could still be a surprise in store with the testing poros deas Bas 50 km from the line and especially the final uncategorized climb of the alto Dela the inos Grenadier and EF Pro cycling teams had an opportunity to unsettle and maybe even un leader Primo RIT strategies were already in evidence on the first climb of the day when a group of 34 Riders broke clear mistar had three men up front and there were also a number of potential stage winners in the Breakaway the likes of David de Cruz and guon Martin could also give their General classification positions a major boost it was a case of two races in one the first the stage Victory up for grabs among the Breakaway and the second the favorites for the overall race win who might also get themselves in the mix for the stage win at the top of the day second climb the alto de San Miguel de balero the lead has had three minutes on the pelaton headed by ritch's jumbo visma team G Martin had already wrapped up the king of the mountains Jersey so no real contest for the day's points but the French Rider second on stage five clearly had Ambitions of going one better 2 hours into the stage and the average speed was still up near 40 km an hour jumbo visma Robert hessing kept the tempo high at the front of the pelaton to ensure the Breakaway group's lead didn't become unmanageable as they began the climb of the alto de Christal less than 80 km from the L the mistar team moved to the front of the peleton heading into the final 50 km as expected Mark Solair accelerated away 29 km from the summit as the Breakaway group started to Splinter one by one aiti then bed this time as he waited for cair to try and bring his mistar teammate into the Breakaway group The pelaton was now less than 2 minutes from the leaders Solera eventually joined the front group 19 km from the line before Gino Med Mark Donovan and yon iseri launched an attack they quickly built a lead of some 40 seconds on the Breakaway group enough to convince The Paton to not continue the Chase and that Advantage was up to 3 minutes at the foot of the alto de latia with the climb getting ever steeper isigi tried twice to attack but David godu managed to reel him in before moving clear himself with four kilometers to go further down the road hu carthy attacked but Richard carath had the legs of the British Rider and the Ecuadorian pulled away with just 3 km remaining goou though was not for catching the group armor fdj Ryder claiming his second stage win of this year's Welter meanwhile rogich was under pressure it's about how much time Richard kater pass can take what is the gap going to be he crosses head at 234 so Primos RIT now has got 45 seconds to get there primas RIT Saves lter by around 19 20 seconds so it was an intense battle right to the end with kath's attack almost changing things at the top of the GC a day before the finish in Madrid it was a bold effort by the Ecuadorian but he was only able to claw back around 20 seconds on Rog glit meaning he fell just short in his bid to snatch the red jersey that belongs to pre much rage for the second successive year after winning stage 11 David godou doubles up with a prestigious win which also gave him a major boost in the general classification I said to myself why not today why not me the weather conditions are those that I like it's good when it's cold and rainy for me it's one of those days when you don't have to think about things you just do everything by Instinct days like this are simply exceptional so Primo's RIT will have an enjoyable Sunday in Madrid thanks to that 24 second lead over carath it's a first Podium finish on a major tour for Hugh karthy of the EF Pro cycling team pretty much Rit also takes the points classification of this 2020 Welter gon Maran wasn't able to get the stage win but the Frenchman can console himself with the king of the mountains Jersey and enrik mass is the best young Rider on the Belta with godu moving ahead of Rush's Alexander flov thanks to his stage win so tomorrow RIT center stage on the route to Madrid that will likely end with a Sprint finish before everyone gets a chance to put their feet up
